# Experimental Report: Automated Membrane Synthesis via NIPS

## Experiment #18

A polysulfone membrane was synthesized using an automated non-solvent-induced phase separation (NIPS) process. The polymer solution was prepared at 13.0% w/w polysulfone concentration in PolarClean solvent. The solution was mixed using the robotic system with heating at 50??C via heating block. After preparation, the solution was rested in a sample vial for 1 day before processing.

The membrane casting was performed under high humidity conditions (96% RH). The polymer solution was automatically drop-cast onto the substrate and then blade-cast. Immediately following blade casting (0.0 min wait time), the sample was immersed in the NIPS bath. No nitrogen flow was applied either from the side during drop-casting or after blade-casting.

The entire synthesis process was fully automated using the robotic system, demonstrating the capability for reproducible membrane fabrication under controlled conditions.
